label: Compiler
description: A translator that converts high-level programming language into machine language, analyzes the complete source program, compiles it, and returns all errors to users.

label: High-level Programming Language
description: Language written by the developer or user (e.g., C, C++, Java), generally in English language.

label: Machine Language
description: Binary language, generally in 0101 format, understood by the processor, also called target code or binary language.

label: Source Program
description: The initial programming code provided by a developer that is processed by the compiler.

label: Target Program
description: The machine language output resulting from the compilation of a source program.

label: Phases of Compiler
description: A total of six sequential steps (Lexical Analyzer, Syntax Analyzer, Semantic Analyzer, Intermediate Code Generator, Code Optimizer, and Code Generator) used to convert a source program into a target program.

label: Lexical Analyzer
description: The first phase of the compiler that takes source code as input, reads it character by character from left to right, and converts it into meaningful lexemes or tokens.

label: Lexemes
description: Meaningful sequences of characters derived from the source code by the lexical analyzer.

label: Tokens
description: Collections of lexemes, which represent identified elements like operators, identifiers, or constants in the source program.

label: Syntax Analyzer
description: The second phase of the compiler, also called parsing, that takes tokens as input, checks the syntax in the program as per programming language rules, and generates a parse tree.

label: Parsing
description: Another name for the Syntax Analysis phase, where the syntax in the program is checked.

label: Parse Tree
description: A hierarchical representation generated by the syntax analysis phase to check for syntax errors.

label: Semantic Analyzer
description: The third phase of the compiler that takes syntax tree and symbol table as input, checks the meaning of each and every line in the program, and tracks identifiers, their types, and expressions.

label: Intermediate Code Generator
description: The fourth phase of the compiler that generates an intermediate representation of the source program, also called three-address code, which is between high-level and machine-level language and is easy to translate.

label: Three Address Code
description: Another name for the intermediate code generated by the Intermediate Code Generator phase.

label: Code Optimizer
description: The fifth and optional phase of the compiler that optimizes the intermediate code by removing unnecessary lines and unused variables for faster execution and less memory space.

label: Code Generation
description: The sixth and final phase of the compiler that takes optimized intermediate code as input, translates it into machine code, and allocates proper memory space and registers to the program.

label: Symbol Table
description: The main data structure of the compiler that stores all information (constants, string values, function names, variable names, labels) in a tabular format for easy fetching.

label: Error Handler
description: A routine that handles errors present in different compiler phases, displays all errors to the users, and allows them to solve and recompile the program.

label: Errors
description: Issues or mistakes detected during the compilation process, categorized by the phase in which they occur, such as wrong spelling tokens, wrong syntax, mismatched operands, or undeclared identifiers.

label: Optimized Intermediate Code
description: The enhanced version of intermediate code produced by the Code Optimizer phase, which requires less memory and faster execution.
